message: Hey team — getting build failures across the board. Artifactory is returning 503s. Anyone else seeing this?
Yes! All our Harness pipelines are failing at the artifact pull step. Looks like it started around 8:00 AM.
Confirmed. jfrog.company.internal is completely unreachable. curl is timing out. This is firm-wide, not just our team.
I'm raising this to JFrog infra team now. @Marcus Webb can you check the ServiceNow ticket? P1 severity — all CI is blocked.
Our release train was supposed to deploy at 8:30. That's not happening. Three squads are now blocked: Payments, Auth, and Data Platform.
Ticket is up: INC0043821. JFrog team says they're investigating storage issues on the primary node. No ETA yet.
I can see in Dynatrace — Artifactory disk usage hit 100% at 07:58 UTC. Storage volume exhausted. This is a full outage.
JFrog infra team is on a bridge call. Root cause confirmed: log rotation job failed, cache filled the 2TB volume. Expanding storage now — ETA 45 minutes.
Update from JFrog: storage volume resize complete, now running fsck. Services should restart in ~25-30 min. Ticket ETA updated to 08:55 UTC.
All Harness pipelines queued and paused. We'll auto-trigger retries once Artifactory is back. No manual intervention needed.
Artifactory is BACK. Pipelines are running. First build completed successfully at 09:00 UTC. 🟢
Confirmed — all queued pipelines running. Payments deploy just kicked off. Auth and Data Platform also recovering.
Dynatrace is green across the board. Artifactory disk at 48% on the new 4TB volume. Permanent fixes applied: stale lock file cleared, logrotate re-enabled, 80% disk alert configured.
Incident resolved at 09:00 UTC. Total duration: 62 minutes. Closing INC0043821. Post-mortem scheduled for Monday. Great work everyone — fast response under pressure.
